result:
If I could change one thing, I think I'd want humans to truly feel the interconnectedness of all living things - not just know it intellectually, but experience it viscerally. The way a parent feels their child's joy or pain as their own, but extended outward to other people, other species, ecosystems.

Not in an overwhelming way that would paralyze anyone, but just enough that causing unnecessary harm would feel immediately wrong, the way putting your hand on a hot stove does. And enough that witnessing flourishing - a forest regrowing, a person learning to read, a conflict resolved - would bring the same deep satisfaction as watching something you planted bloom.

I think so many problems stem from our ability to abstract away the suffering we cause or allow. If that feedback loop was tighter, more immediate, more felt - maybe that would shift things.

What would you change?

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
